title: What Scripture Actually Means by "It's Not Good to Be Alone"
caption:
Genesis 2:18 gets pulled out at every wedding, like solitude only gets solved by a ring. God said it before Eve existed. Before marriage existed. He was diagnosing something bigger than being single.

Isolation feels like strength until it isn't. You call it independence, but nobody's around to catch what you can't see in yourself, or name the thing before it becomes a habit. The guy who answers to no one isn't free. He's unaccountable. Alone with your sin just means more room to hide it.

You don't need a crowd. You need two. Not a group chat, not a podcast host who doesn't know your name. Two or three guys who actually have your number, and use it.

Two are better than one, because if one falls, the other helps him up. (Ecclesiastes 4:9-10)

Text one guy the truth tonight. Start with one line: "Can I tell you something I've been sitting on?" The brother you don't have yet starts with the text you haven't sent yet.

#faith #christianmen #brotherhood #godlymen #manofgod #scripture #community
